query_text: [POST] Bruno Manuel: Respect to the tribe.  I’m focused on self-improvement, building leverage income, and mentoring others to level up body, mind, and finances.  My goal is $10K/month and gain freedom.  For those ahead on the path what’s the biggest lesson that accelerated your growth?34Last comment Feb 19
assistant: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

Use this community to remind yourself of the people you'll have around you when you make it to the other side and don't lose faith.A lot of us are living here already and we have fulfilled the dream we were shooting for... now we are building together towards a higher aim and I can tell you there's nothing better than making your dreams come true with effort, sacrifice and dedication.

query_text:
[POST] J'son Elizondo: 𝗔𝗶 𝗦𝗹𝗼𝗽.. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀..
Yeah I said it Synthesizer is being filled with articles written by BOTS! 🤖
Funniest thing I've seen is 2 articles back to back written by two separate people, same message almost the exact wording. (Not funny at all actually)
The information in this place used to be 𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲, 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗮𝘄..

But now its being littered with em dashes, regurgitated nonsense and oh yea my favorite one, intellectual parroting (sarcasm).
The dead internet theory is a concept predicting that the internet will become predominantly composed of AI-generated content and bot activity, rather than human interaction..
The sad part is that WE are spreading this ai slop. (Literally doing the bid of AI).
By increasing the pace at which it swallows, LITERALLY engulfs the internet whole.
We are masquerading.
Devaluing our own authenticity.
Impersonating what it's like to be a human.
I want to share that community is built on connection and connection is real and raw.
And RAW makes mistakes. Those mistakes in the moment to you, might seem like flaws or blemishes that should be covered. But I want to argue that it is those blemishes are what will distinguish YOU from all of the other articles, posts and NOW 𝗔𝗜- 𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗟𝗢𝗣 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗦.. <-- (Can you believe that ?!)
⭐Studies now show that we even speak like ai ⭐

"Not only is this not x, But its Y"
"Its not x but Y"
And words like DELVE 🤣😆😂
The Rule of Threes. 𝗘𝘅. ( Clear, concise, and compelling) (I DONT even feel right using the rule of threes in formulating my writing because I think people will perceive it as AI. (Even though I speak like that naturally as a philosophy reader)
Its naturally rolling off of our tongues at this point because we consume it SO OFTEN.
ONLY increasing the very PROBLEM that this community aims to solve in the first place..
🌊"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"🌊
With every article generated by ai you are effectively adding MORE water to the already filled bucket.
I want to share with you that not polishing an article, post or comments is actually going to 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘆𝗼𝘂, 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂.
🤮Articles on social media migration and social media rebellion states: "THEY ARE LEAVING BECAUSE IT NO LONGER 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟" SAYING IT 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗟, 𝗨𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗣𝗨𝗟𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗.. "IT FEELS LIKE A 𝗙𝗔𝗞𝗘 𝗘𝗡𝗩𝗜𝗢𝗥𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗖𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗕𝗬 𝗔 𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘" ..🤮
So with all that in mind..
Think before you use ai.
Think before you let it write your articles for you.
And for god sakes don't let it write your whole program from conception to birth, so you can COLD pitch it to someone FOR money.
𝗧𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘆. 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗺.
A NOT so clever scam that aims to take advantage of people who are in pain and need experts, 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗦𝗬𝗡𝗧𝗛𝗘𝗦𝗜𝗭𝗘𝗥𝗦, 𝗡𝗢𝗧 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘀.
Authenticity over curation
Authenticity over curation
say it with me now
Authenticity over curation
"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"
assistant:
Bro, I agree completely. I am a writter at heart and you and I have this beautiful obsession with knowledge and understanding, and I know you draw as much satisfaction from teaching and sharing with others your knowledge as I do... and it is quite literally like a drug for us... but in a good sense, because we get to connect very deeply with amazing people and we get to touch their lives in very impactful ways changing it for the better.
I'm really glad you are bringing this to people's attention because this is something I ALWAYS say to people: If you let AI write for you... I mean... can you really take pride on what "you wrote"? It's not even your own knowledge... and it's sad... because by teaching we solidify our knowledge.... by teaching we learn at the same time.
This is what we are here for and letting something as beautiful as that die would be a true shame.
At the same time... I don't think the AI slop is so bad. It's actually WONDERFUL! Because the more of it there is out there the more we will stand out for being authentically ourseleves and the more rare we'll be. We'll be the only ones willing to do the "hard work" of writting and teaching ourselves and we will benefit greatly personally from that.
The work works more on you than you work on it, and that's a fact, so when people chose the easy path, the path of least resistance they are missing out on the amazing gift that focus and deep work bring to their lives, the development of their discipline, the development of their capacity to actually sit in presence on the present moment and give their best to something beautiful and meaningful like it is connecting with people and helping them change their lives.
I truly loved this post. Thank you so much for sharing it with me, brother... and above all: thank you for being you, Jason, you are a shinning lighthouse in the middle of this dark sea and you have no idea of how deeply appreciated you are, man.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion, but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

query_text:
[POST] Jack Shiller: Sometimes I feel scared of making a banger post to a community if I know that it could fade away into the feed and get forgotten 👶
Yet in many ways, I kinda like that.
On Skool, and especially in Synthesizer, posting to the community feels like it’s based off of the unique energy of the day.
It’s less about how polished the writing is, and more about how attuned it is to the community atmosphere at any given moment.
I try not to be excessive with my moderator powers here in Synthesizer lmao but..
Thought leadership posts fail, honest call-to-action questions are appreciated, and posts that spark conversation work best.
These guidelines help us focus on what’s in front of us TODAY and NOW. Not on what’s in the future.
But it makes me wonder what we can do with those posts that we're proud of, once they've been swept away into the feed.
Asking u guys...
💭 Do you repurpose any of your Skool posts for other content?
assistant:
The work works more on you than you work on it... by simply writing it you are solidifying the knowledge in your mind and the more you do it the stronger those core teachings become to your toolkit of critical knowledge to deply and help people and more people will see it the more persistent your are in your endeavour of spreading such message and you will be extremely persistent if you truly believe that such knowledge has transformed your life and can potentially transform others.
So I'm quite detatched from the outcome of the process of me sharing my knowledge, I simply do it and if nobody listens that's fine... eventually the right person will come across it if I just keep putting it out there and will benefit massively from it and it'll all be worth it in the end.
That's my approach to it.

query_text: [POST] Harry Montford: We have 4 main deep work archetypes.  These are like the 4 classes you can pick inside of a video game. Each have their own benefits, buffs and weaknesses.  They are: - Monastic: 1 main task that you work on for most of the day, minimal breaks. - Bidomal: Long deep work days, but also complete rest days inside the week.  - Rhythmic: Deep work sessions in the morning, shallow work in the afternoon -> evening.  - Journalistic: No clear structure, one minute you could be partying in Bali, the next you are recording          a YT video.  My personal favourite is Rhythmic, but I also know many people who prefer the Monastic work type.  SO here's my question, what would do you follow and which one would you LIKE to try?
assistant: Bro, I'm in Bali but I never do the Journalist style, lol 😂.For me is either Monastic or Rhythmic and I honestly prefer Monastic but it's truly an art and quite a skill to develop.

Talk to people Send 20 messages Goal is 3-5 real responses  3. Post daily Even simple posts build momentum  4. Improve based on feedback Not on your own ideas  5. Repeat Every week, not once  That’s it No results = no volume More action = more feedback = better results Start now Fix later

[POST] Kasparas Stancikas: Just before i tell you about the craziest 30 days of my life, i want to thank every single one of you for keeping me accountable under my daily accountability posts:)))

THE STORY:
34 days ago my great friend told me:
"Kasparas, in a month there’s this amateur kickboxing competition."
2 seconds later, I said NO because right now i am:
Following synthesizer roadmap and thats my nr1 priority in life
i want to put all my focus into my business

I got a response: “Only if you promise to train really damn hard.”

I quit bodybuilding and went all in on martial arts training 1-2 times a day and keeping myself accountable in synthesizer every single day for 30 days straight sharing how i am prepping for my fight + scaling my business all at the same time

My trainer had no clue, and it all worked out.

Round 3: I had zero cardio left, luckily my opponent was hella gassed out too. This round was just to see who could survive the longest. And guess what? With only 10 seconds left, I landed a clear hook and KNOCKED HIM OUT, winning the god damn fight!
I still remember my friends screaming in the corner. (Watch the vid below.)

I grew so much as a person, gained so much wisdom, and even managet to CLOSE MULTIPLE HIGH TICKET deals during the prep.
I proved to myself that ANYTHING is possible. If you really want it bad enough you can scale your business and chase your other wild dreams all at the same time

query_text: [POST] Paul Hedron: The basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual.  By understanding the individual, a leader has better command of complexity.  An individual has a capable skillset, and limitations. Understanding both the capability/capacity,

Instead of posting random self-improvement content, I’m building a structured flow:  1. A problem people recognize immediately 2. A guided reflection 3. A daily actionable step 4. Consistency over motivation  The goal isn’t engagement. The goal is clarity + behavior change.  Right now I’m testing how people respond when the focus shifts from inspiration → guidance  Not selling anything here — just observing behavior patterns and learning what actually helps people follow through in real life. game).This is a clear breakdown of it with some estimate projections that reflect this point:📊 Success (Completion) Rates by Program TypeProgram TypeTypical Completion Rate (Success Proxy)Free programs~5–15 % complete Low-ticket programs (e.g., paid but no heavy support)~15–40 % complete High-ticket programs (e.g., with coaching/community support)~60–80 %+ completeI actually took the time query_text:
[POST] Tom Stoll: would you agree?
yeas
no (why in comments)
other (also in comments)
27 votes
assistant:
I agree completely.
First thing to understand is where people are amongst the 3 awareness levels.
Are they?
1-No awareness (they are in pain but don't know what's their problem).
2-Problem aware (they know they are in pain and know at least on a high level view what's their problem and starting to activeley look for solutions).
3-Solution awareness (know they have a problem and know what's the solution, now they are ready to take action on it).
Level 2 and 3 are the best parts.
That's the warmest leads you'll get.
Funnel structure:
1-At level one you call out their pain and attract them to your community (or YT channel or whatever).
2-At level 2 you get them inside your community and give them declarative knowledge so they start seeing what's their "enemy", their problem.
3-At level 3 you present them with the solution and they are ready to start taking actionable steps in order to solve it, usually this is best done on a sales' call already.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

query_text:
[POST] Christy Sanderson: There was a time when I could articulate powerful ideas.
Clarity. Discipline. Identity. Structure. Consistency.
I could write about them. Teach them. Quote them.
But there was a quiet gap between what I said and what I had actually tested under pressure.
I remember one particular week — overwhelmed, financially stretched, emotionally tired. I had been speaking about consistency and structure publicly… but privately, my own systems were fragile.
That’s when it hit me.
It’s easy to repost wisdom when life is calm.
It’s different to live it when life pushes back.
My pain point wasn’t hypocrisy.
It was realization.
I had absorbed powerful ideas from books, podcasts, communities — and I believed them. But belief is not embodiment.
Pressure reveals what you’ve actually integrated.
So I made a decision.
I stopped sharing ideas I hadn’t personally run through fire.
I stopped echoing what sounded impressive.
I started asking:
Does this hold up when I’m tired?
Does this work when money is tight?
Does this help when doubt creeps in?
Does this produce results consistently?
And something changed.
My voice got quieter — but stronger.
My content got simpler — but sharper.
Because now, when I say something works, it’s not because it sounds right.
It’s because I’ve watched it survive stress.
There’s a difference between information and proof.
Reposting spreads ideas.
Pressure-testing builds authority.
And authority built through lived experience can’t be shaken by trends.
What idea have you personally pressure-tested that most people only repost?
assistant:
Raw action solves it all.
Goal -> Daily actionable plan (reverse engineered) -> Outputs -> Feedback and reiteration (tracking)
Also understanding yourself and how your mind works makes a massive difference because of the following:
Experiences create beliefs, beliefs conform your identity (there are three kinds of beliefs but I'm not getting into that now, ask @Ethan Shahan lol 😂), your identity builds your paradigm, your paradigm (the lenses through which you actually observe components of reality [tangible and intangible]) will inform your percieved options and from your percieved options you will make a decision and that decision will turn into action, enough action will install on your unconscious mind a certain program as a behaviour solidifying it (not permanently, neuroplasticity is a thing), with enough awareness you can have a saying to make such behaviour (routine) intentional or unintentional.
Thank you for this post. I really appreciate it.
I wouldn't have believed all of this if I hadn't tested it myself and I am so fucking glad I have always been a mad scientist of sorts running experiements on myself and choosing to try things out to see if they actually work. To it I owe my success.
